definition.description: A number represented as a series of ASCII numeric characters consisting of an optional leading sign (+ or -), the digits and an optional decimal point. In the absence of a sign, the number is assumed to be positive. If there is no decimal point the number is assumed to be an integer.Examples: |999| |-123.792|

data_types.description: Specifies the interval between the lowest and the highest values in a series of data. In the case where a numeric range is unbounded on one side, the component of the unbounded side is null. Whether the end points are included in the range is defined in the usage note for the field.

definition.name: Numeric Range
definition.description: Specifies the interval between the lowest and the highest values in a series of data. In the case where a numeric range is unbounded on one side, the component of the unbounded side is null. Whether the end points are included in the range is defined in the usage note for the field.

fields.name: Low Value
fields.description: The number specifying the lower limit or boundary of the range.

fields.name: High Value
fields.description: The number specifying the high limit or boundary of the range.

data_types.description: The code and associated date defining a significant event relating to a bill that may affect payer processing. Example: |23&Benefits Exhausted&NUBC^19920108|

definition.name: Occurrence Code and Date
definition.description: The code and associated date defining a significant event relating to a bill that may affect payer processing.Example: |23&Benefits Exhausted&NUBC^19920108|

fields.name: Occurrence Code
fields.description: The NUBC code for the event or occurrence relating to a bill that may affect payer processing.

fields.name: Occurrence Date
fields.description: The date the event, relating to a bill that may affect payer processing, occurred.

data_types.description: This data type specifies a fully coded version for forming a relationship between an order and one or more other orders. The relationship may be sequential or a cyclical pattern.

definition.name: Order Sequence Definition
definition.description: This data type specifies a fully coded version for forming a relationship between an order and one or more other orders. The relationship may be sequential or a cyclical pattern.

fields.name: Sequence/Results Flag
fields.description: Identifies whether sequence conditions or a repeating cycle of orders is defined. Refer to HL7-defined Table 0524 - Sequence condition for valid values.

fields.name: Placer Order Number: Entity Identifier
fields.description: Contains the first component of the placer order number, entity identifier .

fields.name: Placer Order Number: Namespace Id
fields.description: Contains the second component of the placer order number, namespace ID. Refer to user-defined table 0363 - Assigning Authority for suggested values.

fields.name: Filler Order Number: Entity Identifier
fields.description: Contains the first component of the filler order number, entity identifier.

fields.name: Filler Order Number: Namespace Id
fields.description: Contains the second component of the filler order number, namespace ID. Refer to user-defined table 0363 - Assigning Authority for suggested values

fields.name: Sequence Condition Value
fields.description: Defines the relationship between the start/end of the related predecessor or successor order and the current order from ORC-2, 3 or 4.

fields.name: Maximum Number Of Repeats
fields.description: The maximum number of repeats to be used only on cyclic groups. The total number of repeats is constrained by the end date/time of the last repeat or the end date/time of the parent, whichever is first.

fields.name: Placer Order Number: Universal Id
fields.description: Contains the next to the last component of the placer order number, universal ID.

fields.name: Placer Order Number: Universal Id Type
fields.description: Contains the last component of the placer order number. Refer to HL7 table 0301 - Universal ID Type for valid values.

fields.name: Filler Order Number: Universal Id
fields.description: Contains the next to the last component of the filler order number, universal ID.

fields.name: Filler Order Number: Universal Id Type
fields.description: Contains the last component of the placer order number. Refer to HL7 table 0301 - Universal ID Type for valid values.

data_types.description: A code and the related dates that identify an event that relates to the payment of the claim. For example, Prior Stay Dates which is the from/through dates given by the patient of any hospital stay that ended within 60 days of this hospital or SNF admission. Example: |71&Prior Stay Date&NUBC^20030106^20030107|

definition.name: Occurrence Span Code and Date
definition.description: A code and the related dates that identify an event that relates to the payment of the claim. For example, Prior Stay Dates which is the from/through dates given by the patient of any hospital stay that ended within 60 days of this hospital or SNF admission. Example: |71&Prior Stay Date&NUBC^20030106^20030107|

fields.name: Occurrence Span Code
fields.description: The date an event started that relates to the payment of a claim.

fields.name: Occurrence Span Start Date
fields.description: The date an event ended that relates to the payment of a claim.

data_types.description: This data type specifies the institutional privileges with associated detail granted to a provider.

definition.name: Practitioner Institutional Privileges
definition.description: This data type specifies the institutional privileges with associated detail granted to a provider.

fields.name: Privilege
fields.description: Specifies the institutional privilege itself. Refer to user-defined table 0525 - Privilege for suggested values.

fields.name: Privilege Class
fields.description: Specifies the class category of institutional privilege. Refer to User-Defined Table 0526 - Privilege Class for suggested values.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.description: Specifies the date the institutional privilege is/was no longer valid.

fields.name: Activation Date
fields.description: Specifies the date the institutional privilege became/becomes valid.

fields.name: Facility
fields.description: Specifies the facility in which the institutional privilege is/was valid.

data_types.description: This data type is used to specify a patient location within a healthcare institution. Which components are valued depends on the needs of the site. For example for a patient treated at home, only the person location type is valued. It is most commonly used for specifying patient locations, but may refer to other types of persons within a healthcare setting. Example: Nursing Unit A nursing unit at Community Hospital: 4 East, room 136, bed B  4E^136^B^CommunityHospital^^N^^^ A clinic at University Hospitals: Internal Medicine Clinic located in the Briones building, 3rd floor. InternalMedicine^^^UniversityHospitals^^C^Briones^3^

definition.name: Person Location
definition.description: This data type is used to specify a patient location within a healthcare institution. Which components are valued depends on the needs of the site. For example for a patient treated at home, only the person location type is valued. It is most commonly used for specifying patient locations, but may refer to other types of persons within a healthcare setting.Example: Nursing Unit A nursing unit at Community Hospital: 4 East, room 136, bed B  4E^136^B^CommunityHospital^^N^^^A clinic at University Hospitals: Internal Medicine Clinic located in the Briones building, 3rd floor. InternalMedicine^^^UniversityHospitals^^C^Briones^3^

fields.name: Point Of Care
fields.description: This component specifies the code for the point where patient care is administered. It is conditional on PL.6 Person Location Type (e.g., nursing unit or department or clinic). After floor, it is the most general patient location designation. Refer to User-defined Table 0302 - Point of care for suggested values.

fields.name: Room
fields.description: This component specifies the code for the patient's room. After point of care, it is the most general person location designation. Refer to User-defined Table 0303 - Room for suggested values.

fields.name: Bed
fields.description: This component specifies the code for the patient's bed. After room, it is the most general person location designation. Refer to User-defined Table 0304 - Bed for suggested values.

fields.name: Facility
fields.description: This component is subject to site interpretation but generally describes the highest level physical designation of an institution, medical center or enterprise. It is the most general person location designation.

fields.name: Location Status
fields.description: This component specifies the code for the status or availability of the location. For example, it may convey bed status. Refer to User-defined Table 0306 - Location status for suggested values.

fields.name: Person Location Type
fields.description: Person location type is the categorization of the persons location defined by facility, building, floor, point of care, room or bed. Although not a required field, when used, it may be the only populated field. It usually includes values such as nursing unit, department, clinic, SNF, physicians office. Refer to User-defined Table 0305 - Person location type for suggested values.

fields.name: Building
fields.description: This component specifies the code for the building where the person is located. After facility, it is the most general person location designation. Refer to User-defined Table 0307 - Building for suggested values.

fields.name: Floor
fields.description: This component specifies the code for the floor where the person is located. After building, it is the most general person location designation. Refer to User-defined Table 0308 - Floor for suggested values.

fields.name: Location Description
fields.description: This component describes the location in free text.

fields.name: Comprehensive Location Identifier
fields.description: The unique identifier that represents the physical location as a whole without regard for the individual components. This accommodates sites that may have a different method of defining physical units or who may code at a less granular level. For example, point of care, room, and bed may be 1 indivisible code.

fields.name: Assigning Authority For Location
fields.description: The entity that creates the data for the individual physical location components. If populated, it should be the authority for all components populated. Refer to User-defined Table 0363 - Assigning authority for suggested values for the first sub-component of the HD component, <namespace ID>.

data_types.type_name: Practitioner License or Other ID Number
data_types.description: This data type specifies a practitioners license number, or other ID number such as UPIN, Medicare and Medicaid number, and associated detail.

definition.name: Practitioner License or Other ID Number
definition.description: This data type specifies a practitioners license number, or other ID number such as UPIN, Medicare and Medicaid number, and associated detail.

fields.name: Id Number
fields.description: Specifies the license number or other ID number such as UPIN, Medicare and Medicaid number.

fields.name: Type Of Id Number
fields.description: Specifies the type of number.

fields.name: State/Other Qualifying Information
fields.description: Specifies the state or province in which the license or ID is valid, if relevant, or other qualifying information. It is recommended that state qualifications use the abbreviations from the postal service of the country.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.description: Specifies the date when the license or ID is no longer valid.

data_types.description: This data type is the equivalent of an XCN data type joined with a TS data type. However, the XCN data type has been flattened to allow legal expression of its embedded complex data types HD, TS, CE and CWE.

definition.name: Performing Person Time Stamp
definition.description: This data type is the equivalent of an XCN data type joined with a TS data type. However, the XCN data type has been flattened to allow legal expression of its embedded complex data types HD, TS, CE and CWE.

fields.name: Id Number
fields.description: Coded ID according to a user-defined table, defined by the 8th component. If the first component is present, either the source table or the assigning authority must be valued.

fields.name: Family Name
fields.description: This component allows full specification of the surname of a person. Where appropriate, it differentiates the person's own surname from that of the person's partner or spouse, in cases where the person's name may contain elements from either name. It also permits messages to distinguish the surname prefix (such as "van" or "de") from the surname root. See section 2.A.30.

fields.name: Given Name
fields.description: First name.

fields.name: Second And Further Given Names Or Initials Thereof
fields.description: Multiple middle names may be included by separating them with spaces.

fields.name: Suffix (e.g., Jr Or Iii)
fields.description: Used to specify a name suffix (e.g., Jr. or III).

fields.name: Prefix (e.g., Dr)
fields.description: Used to specify a name prefix (e.g., Dr.).

fields.name: Degree (e.g., Md)
fields.description: Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. See Professional Suffix component.

fields.name: Source Table
fields.description: CN ID source is used as the HL7 identifier for the user-defined table of values for this component. Used to delineate the first component.

fields.name: Assigning Authority
fields.description: The assigning authority is a unique identifier of the system (or organization or agency of department) that creates the data. It is a HD data type. User-defined Table 0363 - Assigning authority is used as the HL7 identifier for the user-defined table of values for the first sub-component of the HD component, <namespace ID>.

fields.name: Name Type Code
fields.description: A code that represents the type of name. Refer to HL7 Table 0200 - Name type for valid values (see Section 2.A.88,  XPN - extended person name ).

fields.name: Identifier Check Digit
fields.description: The check digit in this data type is not an add-on produced by the message processor. It is the check digit that is part of the identifying number used in the sending application. If the sending application does not include a self-generated check digit in the identifying number, this component should be valued null.

fields.name: Check Digit Scheme
fields.description: Contains the code identifying the check digit scheme employed.

fields.name: Identifier Type Code
fields.description: A code corresponding to the type of identifier. In some cases, this code may be used as a qualifier to the "Assigning authority" component. Refer to HL7 Table 0203 - Identifier type for suggested values.

fields.name: Assigning Facility
fields.description: The place or location identifier where the identifier was first assigned to the patient. This component is not an inherent part of the identifier but rather part of the history of the identifier: as part of this data type, its existence is a convenience for certain intercommunicating systems.

fields.name: Date/Time Action Performed
fields.description: This component describes when the activity was performed.

fields.name: Name Context
fields.description: This component is used to designate the context in which a name is used. The main use case is in Australian healthcare: indigenous patients who prefer to use different names when attending different healthcare institutions. Another use case occurs in the US where health practitioners can be licensed under slightly different names and the reporting of the correct name is vital for administrative purposes. Refer to chapter 3, section 3.4.2.6 for more detailed information on how to use this table. Refer to User-defined table 0448 - Name context for suggested values.

fields.name: Name Validity Range
fields.description: This component contains the start and end date/times, which define the period during which this name was valid. See section 2.A.20, " DR - date/time range " for description of subcomponents.Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to PPN.20 Effective Date and PPN.21Expiration Date. This component cannot be fully expressed and was identified as v 2.4 erratum

fields.name: Name Assembly Order
fields.description: A code that represents the preferred display order of the components of this person name. Refer to HL7 Table 0444 - Name assembly order for valid values.

fields.name: Effective Date
fields.description: The first date, if known, on which the address is valid and active.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.description: The last date, if known, on which the address is valid and active.

fields.name: Professional Suffix
fields.description: Used to specify an abbreviation, or a string of abbreviations, denoting qualifications that support the persons profession, (e.g., licenses, certificates, degrees, affiliations with professional societies, etc.). The Professional Suffix normally follows the Family Name when the Person Name is used for display purposes. Please note that this component is an unformatted string and is used for display purposes only. Detailed information regarding the contents of Professional Suffix is obtained using appropriate segments in Chapter 15, Personnel Management.

fields.name: Assigning Jurisdiction
fields.description: The geo-political body that assigned the identifier in component 1.

fields.name: Assigning Agency Or Department
fields.description: The agency or department that assigned the identifier in component 1.

data_types.description: Uniquely identifies the parent results OBX segment related to the current order, together with the information in OBR-29-parent.

definition.name: Parent Result Link
definition.description: Uniquely identifies the parent results OBX segment related to the current order, together with the information in OBR-29-parent.

fields.name: Parent Observation Identifier
fields.description: Contains the unique identifier of the parent observation as defined in the OBX-3 of the parent result. The value is the same as the OBX-3 of the parent.

fields.name: Parent Observation Sub-identifier
fields.description: Contains the sub-ID of the parent result as defined in the OBX-4 of the parent result. The value is the same as the OBX-4 of the parent.

fields.name: Parent Observation Value Descriptor
fields.description: Contains a descriptor of the parent observation value as specified in the OBX-5 of the parent result.

data_types.description: This data type indicates whether to process a message as defined in HL7 Application (level 7) Processing rules.

definition.name: Processing Type
definition.description: This data type indicates whether to process a message as defined in HL7 Application (level 7) Processing rules.

fields.name: Processing Id
fields.description: A value that defines whether the message is part of a production, training, or debugging system. Refer to HL7 Table 0103 - Processing ID for valid values.

fields.name: Processing Mode
fields.description: A value that defines whether the message is part of an archival process or an initial load. Refer to HL7 Table 0207 - Processing mode for valid values.

data_types.description: This data type specifies the policy type and amount covered by the insurance.

definition.name: Policy Type and Amount
definition.description: This data type specifies the policy type and amount covered by the insurance.

fields.name: Policy Type
fields.description: Specifies the policy type.

fields.name: Amount Class
fields.description: Specifies the amount quantity class.

fields.name: Money Or Percentage Quantity
fields.description: Specifies the currency or percentage quantity.Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to PTA.4 instead.

fields.name: Money Or Percentage
fields.description: specifies an amount that may be either currency or a percentage.

data_types.description: This data type contains a segment field name and the list of values to be passed to the query processor. Example: |@PID.5.1^EVANS|

definition.name: Query Input Parameter List
definition.description: This data type contains a segment field name and the list of values to be passed to the query processor.Example: |@PID.5.1^EVANS|

fields.name: Segment Field Name
fields.description: This component contains the segment field name.

fields.name: Values
fields.description: This component contains the field value or values in the form "value1& value2 & value3"

data_types.description: This field indicates the conditions that qualify the rows to be returned in the query response. Note that this field conveys the same information as the "WHERE" clause in the corresponding SQL expression of the query, but is formatted differently. Example: |@PID.5.1^EQ^EVANS|

definition.name: Query Selection Criteria
definition.description: This field indicates the conditions that qualify the rows to be returned in the query response. Note that this field conveys the same information as the "WHERE" clause in the corresponding SQL expression of the query, but is formatted differently.Example: |@PID.5.1^EQ^EVANS|

fields.name: Segment Field Name
fields.description: The name of the field that is participating as a qualifier (usually the "key"). Refer to Section 2.A.59.1,  Segment Field Name (ST) , for segment field name conventions.

fields.name: Value
fields.description: The value to which the field will be compared.

data_types.description: This specifies the format of a column in terms of a segment field name, a data type, and a maximum length. Example: This defines a column containing the value of the "last name" component of PID-5, expressed as a ST data type with a maximum width of 20. |@PID.5.1^ST^20|

definition.name: Row Column Definition
definition.description: This specifies the format of a column in terms of a segment field name, a data type, and a maximum length.Example: This defines a column containing the value of the "last name" component of PID-5, expressed as a ST data type with a maximum width of 20. |@PID.5.1^ST^20|

fields.name: Segment Field Name
fields.description: The HL7 segment field name, which identifies the field occupying the column. Refer to Section 2.A.59.1, "Segment Field Name (ST)," for segment field name definition conventions.

fields.name: Hl7 Data Type
fields.description: The two or three character HL7 data type. Refer to HL7 Table 0440 - Data Types in section 2.16 for valid values.

fields.name: Maximum Column Width
fields.description: The maximum width of the column, as dictated by the responding system. This may vary from the HL7-defined maximum field length.

data_types.description: Describes a reference range and its supporting detail.

definition.name: Reference Range
definition.description: Describes a reference range and its supporting detail.

fields.name: Numeric Range
fields.description: This component specifies the numeric interval of the reference data. Range is taken to be inclusive (i.e., the range includes the end points). Units are context sensitive and are defined in the usage note for the field where this data type is used.

fields.name: Administrative Sex
fields.description: This component specifies which gender for which the reference range is valid. Refer to User-defined Table 0001 - Administrative Sex for suggested values.

fields.name: Age Range
fields.description: This component specifies the age range for which the reference range is valid. Ages of less than one year should be specified as a fraction (e.g., 1 month = 0.0830, 1 week = 0.01920, 1 day = 0.0027300). However, for most purposes involving infants, the gestational age (measured in weeks) is preferred. The lower end of the range is not indicated; the upper end is, assuring that series of ranges do not overlap.

fields.name: Gestational Age Range
fields.description: This component specifies the gestational age range for which the reference range is valid. Gestational age is relevant only when the reference range is influenced by the stage of pregnancy. The gestational age is measured in weeks from conception. For example, |1&4| implies that the normals apply to gestational ages from 1 week to 4 weeks inclusive. The lower end of the range is not included; the upper end is, assuring that series of age ranges do not overlap.

fields.name: Species
fields.description: This component specifies the species for which the reference range is valid. Species is assumed to be human unless otherwise stated. Example values are rabbit, mouse, and rat.

fields.name: Race/Subspecies
fields.description: This component specifies the race or subspecies for which the reference range is valid. In the case of humans (the default species), the race is specified when race influences the reference range. When normal ranges for animals are being described, this component can be used to describe subspecies or special breeds of animals.

fields.name: Conditions
fields.description: This component specifies any arbitrary condition for which the reference range is valid. This may include such conditions as phase of menstrual cycle or dose of a particular drug. It is provided as a way to communicate the normal ranges for special conditions. It does not allow automatic checking of these text conditions.

data_types.description: contains the interval between repeated services.

definition.name: Repeat Interval
definition.description: contains the interval between repeated services.

fields.name: Repeat Pattern
fields.description: The repeating frequency with which the treatment is to be administered. It is similar to the frequency and SIG code tables used in order entry systems.

fields.name: Explicit Time Interval
fields.description: This component explicitly lists the actual times referenced by the code in the first component, in the following format: HHMM,HHMM,HHMM,. This second component will be used to clarify the first component in cases where the actual times vary within an institution. If the time of the order spans more than a single day, this new component is only practical if the same times of administration occur for each day of the order. If the actual start time of the order (as given by the fourth component of the quantity/timing field) is after the first explicit time, the first administration is taken to be the first explicit time after the start time. In the case where the patient moves to a location having a different set of explicit times, the existing order may be updated with a new quantity/timing field showing the changed explicit times.

data_types.description: This data type specifies insurance coverage detail for a room.

definition.name: Room Coverage
definition.description: This data type specifies insurance coverage detail for a room.

fields.name: Room Type
fields.description: Specifies the room type.

fields.name: Amount Type
fields.description: Specifies amount quantity type

fields.name: Coverage Amount
fields.description: Specifies amount covered by the insurance as a currency or percentage quantity.Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to Money or Percentage.

fields.name: Money Or Percentage
fields.description: specifies an amount that may be either currency or a percentage.

data_types.description: This data type transmits information about data stored on another system. It contains a reference pointer that uniquely identifies the data on the other system, the identity of the other system, and the type of data.

definition.name: Reference Pointer
definition.description: This data type transmits information about data stored on another system. It contains a reference pointer that uniquely identifies the data on the other system, the identity of the other system, and the type of data.

fields.name: Pointer
fields.description: A unique key assigned by the system that stores the data. The key, which is a ST data type, is used to identify and access the data.

fields.name: Application Id
fields.description: A unique designator of the system that stores the data. It is a HD data type (See Section 2.A.33, "HD - hierarchic designator"). Application IDs must be unique across a given HL7 implementation.

fields.name: Type Of Data
fields.description: An ID data type that declares the general type of data. Refer to HL7 Table 0191- Type of referenced data for valid values.

fields.name: Subtype
fields.description: An ID data type declaring the format for the data of subcomponent <main type>. Refer to HL7 Table 0291 - Subtype of referenced data for valid values.

data_types.description: The repeat pattern data type should be used where it is necessary to define the frequency at which an event is to take place. This data type provides a way to define repeat pattern codes "on the fly". The repeat pattern code is equivalent to the TQ data type, component 2, sub-component 1 (repeat pattern). The additional components define the meaning of the repeat pattern code. Components 2 - 10 are used to define relatively simple repeat patterns. Component 11 is provided to define complex repeat patterns. This data type forms a bridge between the 2.x Repeat Pattern concept from Quantity/Timing, and the Version 3.0 GTS General Timing Specification. Component 1 is the 2.x concept of repeat pattern. Components 2-7 are derived from the version 3.0 data type PIVL. Components 8-10 are derived from the version 3.0 EIVL data type. If a repeat pattern cannot be defined using components 2-10, then component 11, General Timing Specification is provided. This allows the full literal form of the version 3.0 GTS to be specified. Examples: |Q1H&Every 1 Hour&HL7xxx^^^^1^h| |Q2J2&Every second Tuesday&HL7xxx^DW^2^^2^wk|

definition.name: Repeat Pattern
definition.description: The repeat pattern data type should be used where it is necessary to define the frequency at which an event is to take place. This data type provides a way to define repeat pattern codes "on the fly". The repeat pattern code is equivalent to the TQ data type, component 2, sub-component 1 (repeat pattern). The additional components define the meaning of the repeat pattern code. Components 2 - 10 are used to define relatively simple repeat patterns. Component 11 is provided to define complex repeat patterns. This data type forms a bridge between the 2.x Repeat Pattern concept from Quantity/Timing, and the Version 3.0 GTS General Timing Specification. Component 1 is the 2.x concept of repeat pattern. Components 2-7 are derived from the version 3.0 data type PIVL. Components 8-10 are derived from the version 3.0 EIVL data type. If a repeat pattern cannot be defined using components 2-10, then component 11, General Timing Specification is provided. This allows the full literal form of the version 3.0 GTS to be specified.Examples: |Q1H&Every 1 Hour&HL7xxx^^^^1^h| |Q2J2&Every second Tuesday&HL7xxx^DW^2^^2^wk|

fields.name: Repeat Pattern Code
fields.description: A code representing the repeat pattern defined by the other components of this data type. Refer to User-defined Table 335 - Repeat Pattern for suggested values.

fields.name: Calendar Alignment
fields.description: Specifies an alignment of the repetition to a calendar (e.g., to distinguish every 30 days from the 5th of every month). Refer to HL7 Table 0527 - Calendar Alignment for valid values.

fields.name: Phase Range Begin Value
fields.description: Used for Calendar aligned repeat patterns to determine the amount of time from the beginning of particular RPT-2 (Calendar Alignment) to the beginning of the phase. If Calendar Alignment is DW (days of week), then this would be the offset from the beginning of the week.

fields.name: Phase Range End Value
fields.description: Used for Calendar aligned repeat patterns to determine the amount of time from the beginning of particular RPT-2 (Calendar Alignment) to the end of the phase.

fields.name: Period Quantity
fields.description: A time duration specifying the frequency at which the periodic interval repeats. RPT-6 (Period Units) defines the units of time for this component.

fields.name: Period Units
fields.description: Defines the units used for RPT-5 (Period Quantity). Constrained to units of time. The codes for unit of measure are specified in the Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM) [http://aurora.rg.iupui.edu/UCUM].

fields.name: Institution Specified Time
fields.description: A code that indicates whether the exact timing is up to the party executing the schedule (e.g., to distinguish every 8 hours from 3 times a day.) Refer to HL7 Table 0136 - Yes/No Indicator for valid values.

fields.name: Event
fields.description: A code for a common (periodical) activity of daily living. Refer to HL7 Table 0528, Event-Related Period for valid values.

fields.name: Event Offset Quantity
fields.description: An interval that marks the offsets for the beginning, width and end of the event-related periodic interval measured from the time each such event actually occurred. A positive numeric value indicates the amount of time after the event in RPT-8. A negative numeric value indicates the amount of time prior to the event in RPT-8. RPT-10 (Event Offset Units) defines the units of time for this component.

fields.name: Event Offset Units
fields.description: Defines the units used for RPT-9 (Event Offset Quantity). Constrained to units of time. The codes for unit of measure are specified in the Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM) [http://aurora.rg.iupui.edu/UCUM].

fields.name: General Timing Specification
fields.description: The General Timing Specification as defined by the Version 3 Data Types document.

data_types.description: This data type specifies an entity's street address and associated detail.

definition.name: Street Address
definition.description: This data type specifies an entity's street address and associated detail.

fields.name: Street Or Mailing Address
fields.description: This component specifies the street or mailing address of a person or institution. When referencing an institution, this first component is used to specify the institution name. When used in connection with a person, this component specifies the first line of the address.

data_types.description: This data type is used to communicate parameters and preferences to the filler application regarding the selection of an appropriate time slot, resource, location, or filler override criterion for an appointment.

definition.name: Scheduling Class Value Pair
definition.description: This data type is used to communicate parameters and preferences to the filler application regarding the selection of an appropriate time slot, resource, location, or filler override criterion for an appointment.

fields.name: Parameter Class
fields.description: The first component of this field is a code identifying the parameter or preference being passed to the filler application. Refer to User-defined Table 0294 - Time selection criteria parameter class codes - Time selection criteria parameter class codes for suggested values.

fields.name: Parameter Value
fields.description: The second component is the actual data value for that parameter.

data_types.description: A non-negative integer in the form of a NM field. The uses of this data type are defined in the chapters defining the segments and messages in which it appears. Maximum Length: 4. This allows for a number between 0 and 9999 to be specified

definition.name: Sequence ID
definition.description: A non-negative integer in the form of a NM field. The uses of this data type are defined in the chapters defining the segments and messages in which it appears.Maximum Length: 4. This allows for a number between 0 and 9999 to be specified

data_types.description: The structured numeric data type is used to unambiguously express numeric clinical results along with qualifications. This enables receiving systems to store the components separately, and facilitates the use of numeric database queries. The corresponding sets of values indicated with the <comparator> and <separator/suffix> components are intended to be the authoritative and complete set of values. If additional values are needed for the <comparator> and <separator/suffix> components, they should be submitted to HL7 for inclusion in the Standard. Examples: |>^100|   (greater than 100) |^100^-^200|  (equal to range of 100 through 200)

definition.name: Structured Numeric
definition.description: The structured numeric data type is used to unambiguously express numeric clinical results along with qualifications. This enables receiving systems to store the components separately, and facilitates the use of numeric database queries. The corresponding sets of values indicated with the <comparator> and <separator/suffix> components are intended to be the authoritative and complete set of values. If additional values are needed for the <comparator> and <separator/suffix> components, they should be submitted to HL7 for inclusion in the Standard.Examples: |>^100|   (greater than 100) |^100^-^200|  (equal to range of 100 through 200)

fields.name: Comparator
fields.description: Defined as greater than, less than, greater than or equal, less than or equal, equal, and not equal, respectively (= ">" or "<" or ">=" or "<=" or "=" or "<>"

fields.name: Num1
fields.description: A number.

fields.name: Num2
fields.description: A number or null depending on the measurement.

data_types.description: This data type specifies the practitioners specialty and related information.

definition.name: Specialty Description
definition.description: This data type specifies the practitioners specialty and related information.

fields.name: Specialty Name
fields.description: Identifies the providers specialty.

fields.name: Governing Board
fields.description: Identifies the governing body providing for the specialty.

fields.name: Eligible Or Certified
fields.description: Specifies the certification status. Refer to HL7 Table 0337 - Certification status for valid values.

fields.name: Date Of Certification
fields.description: Specifies when certification occurred.

data_types.description: This data type identifies the site where the specimen should be obtained or where the service should be performed.

definition.name: Specimen Source
definition.description: This data type identifies the site where the specimen should be obtained or where the service should be performed.

fields.name: Specimen Source Name Or Code
fields.description: contains the specimen source name or code (as a CWE data type component). (Even in the case of observations whose name implies the source, a source may be required, e.g., blood culture-heart blood.)

fields.name: Additives
fields.description: identifies an additive introduced to the specimen before or at the time of collection. Refer to HL7 Table0371 - Additive in chapter 7 for valid values. The tables values are taken from NCCLS AUTO4. The value set can be extended with user specific values.

fields.name: Specimen Collection Method
fields.description: describes the method of collection when that information is a part of the order. When the method of collection is logically an observation result, it should be included as a result segment (i.e., OBX segment).

fields.name: Body Site
fields.description: This component specifies the body site from which the specimen was obtained. A nationally recognized coding system is to be used for this field. Valid coding sources for this field include:

fields.name: Site Modifier
fields.description: modifies body site. For example, the site could be antecubital fossa, and the site modifier right. Refer to HL7 Table 0495 Body Site Modifier for allowed values.

fields.name: Collection Method Modifier Code
fields.description: I ndicates whether the specimen is frozen as part of the collection method. Suggested values are F (Frozen); R (Refrigerated). If the component is blank, the specimen is assumed to be at room temperature.

fields.name: Specimen Role
fields.description: indicates the role of the sample. Refer to User-defined Table 0369 - Specimen role for suggested values. Each of these values is normally identifiable by the systems and its components and can influence processing and data management related to the specimen.

data_types.description: Specifies those parameters by which the response will be sorted and by what method. Example: In a tabular response query, where the return data is known by column name, the SRT might look like |LastName^A~FirstName^A|

definition.name: Sort Order
definition.description: Specifies those parameters by which the response will be sorted and by what method.Example: In a tabular response query, where the return data is known by column name, the SRT might look like |LastName^A~FirstName^A|

fields.name: Sort-by Field
fields.description: Identifies the field by which the response will be sorted. In a tabular response, this will be the column name to sort by. In the Segment Pattern and the Display Response, this will be the segment field name to sort by. See QIP in Section 2.A.59.1, "Segment Field Name (ST)" for segment field name definition.

fields.name: Sequencing
fields.description: Identifies how the field or parameter will be sorted; and, if sorted, whether the sort will be case sensitive (the default) or not. Refer to HL7 Table 0397 - Sequencing for valid values

data_types.description: String data is left justified with trailing blanks optional. Any displayable (printable) ACSII characters (hexadecimal values between 20 and 7E, inclusive, or ASCII decimal values between 32 and 126), except the defined escape characters and defined delimiter characters. Example: |almost any data at all| Usage note: The ST data type is intended for short strings (e.g., less than 200 characters). For longer strings the TX or FT data types should be used (see Sections 2.A.78, “TX - text data” or 2.A.31, “FT - formatted text data”).

definition.name: String Data
definition.description: String data is left justified with trailing blanks optional. Any displayable (printable) ACSII characters (hexadecimal values between 20 and 7E, inclusive, or ASCII decimal values between 32 and 126), except the defined escape characters and defined delimiter characters.Example: |almost any data at all| Usage note: The ST data type is intended for short strings (e.g., less than 200 characters). For longer strings the TX or FT data types should be used (see Sections 2.A.78, “TX - text data” or 2.A.31, “FT - formatted text data”).

data_types.description: Specifies the hour of the day with optional minutes, seconds, fraction of second using a 24-hour clock notation and time zone. Format: HH[MM[SS[.S[S[S[S]]]]]][+/-ZZZZ]

definition.name: Time
definition.description: Specifies the hour of the day with optional minutes, seconds, fraction of second using a 24-hour clock notation and time zone.Format: HH[MM[SS[.S[S[S[S]]]]]][+/-ZZZZ]

data_types.description: Describes when a service should be performed and how frequently. Note: The TQ data type is retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to the TQ1 and TQ2 segments defined in chapter 4.

definition.name: Timing Quantity
definition.description: Describes when a service should be performed and how frequently.Note: The TQ data type is retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to the TQ1 and TQ2 segments defined in chapter 4.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.description: This component specifies the quantity of the service that should be provided at each service interval. For example, if two blood cultures are to be obtained every 4 hours, the quantity would be 2. If three units of blood are to be typed and cross-matched, the quantity would be 3. The default value is 1. When units are required, they can be added, specified by a subcomponent delimiter.

fields.name: Interval
fields.description: Determines the interval between repeated services.

fields.name: Duration
fields.description: This component indicates how long the service should continue after it is started. The default is INDEF (do indefinitely). This component is coded as follows:

fields.name: Start Date/Time
fields.description: This component may be specified by the orderer, in which case it indicates the earliest date/time at which the services should be started. In many cases, however, the start date/time will be implied or will be defined by other fields in the order record (e.g., urgency - STAT). In such a case, this field will be empty.

fields.name: End Date/Time
fields.description: When filled in by the requester of the service, this component should contain the latest date/time that the service should be performed. If it has not been performed by the specified time, it should not be performed at all. The requester may not always fill in this value, yet the filling service may fill it in on the basis of the instruction it receives and the actual start time.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: This component describes the urgency of the request. The following values are suggested (the default for Priority is R):

fields.name: Condition
fields.description: This is a free text component that describes the conditions under which the drug is to be given. For example, PRN pain , or to keep blood pressure below 110. The presence of text in this field should be taken to mean that human review is needed to determine the how and/or when this drug should be given.

fields.name: Text
fields.description: This component is a full text version of the instruction (optional).

fields.name: Conjunction
fields.description: This non-null component indicates that a second timing specification is to follow using the repeat delimiter. Refer to HL7 table 0472 - TQ Conjunction ID for valid values

fields.name: Occurrence Duration
fields.description: This component contains the duration for a single performance of a service, e.g., whirlpool twenty minutes three times per day for three days. It is optional within TQ and does not repeat.

fields.name: Total Occurrences
fields.description: This component contains the total number of occurrences of a service that should result from this order. It is optional within TQ and does not repeat. If both the end date/time and the total occurrences are valued and the occurrences would extend beyond the end date/time, then the end date/time takes precedence. Otherwise the number of occurrences takes precedence.

data_types.description: Specifies a point in time. Format: YYYY[MM[DD[HH[MM[SS[.S[S[S[S]]]]]]]]][+/-ZZZZ]^<degree of precision>

definition.name: Time Stamp
definition.description: Specifies a point in time.Format: YYYY[MM[DD[HH[MM[SS[.S[S[S[S]]]]]]]]][+/-ZZZZ]^<degree of precision>

fields.name: Time
fields.description: The point in time.

fields.name: Degree Of Precision
fields.description: Retained only for purposes of backward compatibility as of v 2.3. Refer to component 1 for current method of designating degree of precision. Indicates the degree of precision of the time stamp (Y = year, L = month, D = day, H = hour, M = minute, S = second). Refer to HL7 Table 0529 - Precision for valid value.

data_types.description: String data meant for user display (on a terminal or printer). Such data would not necessarily be left justified since leading spaces may contribute greatly to the clarity of the presentation to the user. Because this type of data is intended for display, it may contain certain escape character sequences designed to control the display. Escape sequence formatting is defined in Section 2.7 "Use of escape sequences in text fields ". Leading spaces should be included. Trailing spaces should be removed. Example: |  leading spaces are allowed.|

definition.name: Text Data
definition.description: String data meant for user display (on a terminal or printer). Such data would not necessarily be left justified since leading spaces may contribute greatly to the clarity of the presentation to the user. Because this type of data is intended for display, it may contain certain escape character sequences designed to control the display. Escape sequence formatting is defined in Section 2.7 "Use of escape sequences in text fields ". Leading spaces should be included. Trailing spaces should be removed.Example: |  leading spaces are allowed.|

data_types.description: A code structure to relate amounts or values to identified data elements necessary to process this claim as qualified by the payer organization.

definition.name: UB Value Code and Amount
definition.description: A code structure to relate amounts or values to identified data elements necessary to process this claim as qualified by the payer organization.

fields.name: Value Code
fields.description: Specifies the National Uniform Billing Committee (NUBC) code itself.

fields.name: Value Amount
fields.description: Specifies the numeric amount when needed to pair with the value.

data_types.description: This data type contains the hours when a patient location is open for visiting. Refer to HL7 Table 0267 - Days of the week for valid values for the first two components.

definition.name: Visiting Hours
definition.description: This data type contains the hours when a patient location is open for visiting. Refer to HL7 Table 0267 - Days of the week for valid values for the first two components.

fields.name: Start Day Range
fields.description: Starting day of visiting hours range. See HL7 Table 0267 - Days of the week for valid values.

fields.name: End Day Range
fields.description: Ending day of visiting hours range. Starting day of visiting hours range. See HL7 Table 0267 - Days of the week for valid values

fields.name: Start Hour Range
fields.description: Starting hour on starting day of visiting hours range. See first component, 2.A.80.1, "Start Day Range (ID)".

fields.name: End Hour Range
fields.description: Ending hour on ending day of visiting hours range See second component, 2.A.80.2, "End Day Range (ID)".

fields.name: Version Id
fields.description: Used to identify the HL7 version. Refer to HL7 Table 0104 - Version ID in section 2.15.9.12 for valid values.

fields.name: Internationalization Code
fields.description: Used to identify the international affiliate country code. The values to be used are those of ISO 3166 -1:1977. The ISO 3166 table has three separate forms of the country code: HL7 specifies that the 3-character (alphabetic) form be used for the country code.

fields.name: International Version Id
fields.description: This field component identifies international affiliates version; it is especially important when the international affiliate has more than a single local version associated with a single US version.

data_types.description: This data type contains the lower bound value and upper bound values that constitute a range. Either or both components may be populated. Example 1  |+^+++| Example 2: Colors of the rainbow |violet^red|

definition.name: Value Range
definition.description: This data type contains the lower bound value and upper bound values that constitute a range. Either or both components may be populated.Example 1  |+^+++| Example 2: Colors of the rainbow |violet^red|

fields.name: First Data Code Value
fields.description: Specifies the lower bound value.

fields.name: Last Data Code Value
fields.description: Specifies the upper bound value.

data_types.description: This data type specifies the number and name of the recording channel where waveform data is transmitted.

definition.name: Channel Identifier
definition.description: This data type specifies the number and name of the recording channel where waveform data is transmitted.

fields.name: Channel Number
fields.description: This component specifies the number of the recording channel.

fields.name: Channel Name
fields.description: This component specifies the name of the recording channel.

data_types.description: This data type identifies the source of the waveform connected to a channel.

definition.name: Waveform Source
definition.description: This data type identifies the source of the waveform connected to a channel.

fields.name: Source One Name
fields.description: This component identifies the first input for the waveform source.

fields.name: Source Two Name
fields.description: This component identifies the second input for the waveform source if a differential input is used.

data_types.description: This data type specifies the address of a person, place or organization plus associated information. Example of usage for US: |1000 Hospital Lane^Ste. 123^Ann Arbor ^MI^99999^USA^B^^WA^|

definition.name: Extended Address
definition.description: This data type specifies the address of a person, place or organization plus associated information.Example of usage for US: |1000 Hospital Lane^Ste. 123^Ann Arbor ^MI^99999^USA^B^^WA^|

fields.name: Other Designation
fields.description: Second line of address. In US usage, it qualifies address. Examples: Suite 555 or Fourth Floor. When referencing an institution, this component specifies the street address.

fields.name: City
fields.description: This component specifies the city, or district or place where the addressee is located depending upon the national convention for formatting addresses for postal usage.

fields.name: State Or Province
fields.description: This component specifies the state or province where the addressee is located. State or province should be represented by the official postal service codes for that country.

fields.name: Zip Or Postal Code
fields.description: This component specifies the zip or postal code where the addressee is located. Zip or postal codes should be represented by the official codes for that country. In the US, the zip code takes the form 99999[-9999], while the Canadian postal code takes the form A9A9A9, and the Australian Postcode takes the form 9999.

fields.name: Country
fields.description: This component specifies the country where the addressee is locatedHL7 specifies that the 3-character (alphabetic) form of ISO 3166 be used for the country code. Refer to HL7 Table 0399 - Country code in section 2.15.9.17 for valid values.

fields.name: Address Type
fields.description: This component specifies the kind or type of address. Refer to HL7 Table 0190 - Address type for valid values.

fields.name: Other Geographic Designation
fields.description: This component specifies any other geographic designation. It includes county, bioregion, SMSA, etc.

fields.name: County/Parish Code
fields.description: A code that represents the county in which the specified address resides. User-defined Table 0289 - County/parish is used as the HL7 identifier for the user-defined table of values for this component. When this component is used to represent the county (or parish), component 8 <other geographic designation> should not duplicate it (i.e., the use of <other geographic designation> to represent the county is allowed only for the purpose of backward compatibility, and should be discouraged in this and future versions of HL7).

fields.name: Census Tract
fields.description: A code that represents the census tract in which the specified address resides. User-defined Table 0288 - Census tract is used as the HL7 identifier for the user-defined table of values for this component.

fields.name: Address Representation Code
fields.description: Different <name/address types> and representations of the same name/address should be described by repeating of this field, with different values of the <name/address type> and/or <name/address representation> component.

fields.name: Address Validity Range
fields.description: This component cannot be fully expressed. Identified as v 2.4 erratum. Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to Effective Date and Expiration Date components.This component contains the start and end date/times, which define the period in which this address was valid.

fields.name: Effective Date
fields.description: The first date, if known, on which the address is valid and active.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.description: The last date, if known, on which the address is valid and active.

data_types.type_name: Extended Composite ID Number and Name for Persons
data_types.description: Example without assigning authority and assigning facility: |1234567^Everyman^Adam^A^III^DR^PHD^ADT01^^L^4^M11^MR| Examples with assigning authority and assigning facility: |12188^Hippocrates^Harold^H^IV^Dr^MD^^&Provider Master.Community Health and Hospitals&L^L^9^M10^DN^&Good Health Hospital.Community Health and Hospitals&L^A|

definition.name: Extended Composite ID Number and Name for Persons
definition.description: Example without assigning authority and assigning facility: |1234567^Everyman^Adam^A^III^DR^PHD^ADT01^^L^4^M11^MR| Examples with assigning authority and assigning facility: |12188^Hippocrates^Harold^H^IV^Dr^MD^^&Provider Master.Community Health and Hospitals&L^L^9^M10^DN^&Good Health Hospital.Community Health and Hospitals&L^A|

fields.name: Id Number
fields.description: This string refers to the coded ID according to a user-defined table, defined by the 9th component. If the first component is present, either the source table or the assigning authority must be valued.

fields.name: Family Name
fields.description: This component allows full specification of the surname of a person. Where appropriate, it differentiates the person's own surname from that of the person's partner or spouse, in cases where the person's name may contain elements from either name. It also permits messages to distinguish the surname prefix (such as "van" or "de") from the surname root. See section 2.A.30, " FN - family name".

fields.name: Given Name
fields.description: First name.

fields.name: Second And Further Given Names Or Initials Thereof
fields.description: Multiple middle names may be included by separating them with spaces.

fields.name: Suffix (e.g., Jr Or Iii)
fields.description: Used to specify a name suffix (e.g., Jr. or III).

fields.name: Prefix (e.g., Dr)
fields.description: Used to specify a name prefix (e.g., Dr.).

fields.name: Degree (e.g., Md)
fields.description: Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. See Professional Suffix component.Used to specify an educational degree (e.g., MD). Refer to User-defined Table 0360 – Degree for suggested values.

fields.name: Source Table
fields.description: CN ID source is used as the HL7 identifier for the user-defined table of values for this component. Used to delineate the first component.

fields.name: Assigning Authority
fields.description: The assigning authority is a unique identifier of the system (or organization or agency of department) that creates the data. User-defined Table 0363 - Assigning authority is used as the HL7 identifier for the user-defined table of values for the first sub-component of the HD component, <namespace ID>.

fields.name: Name Type Code
fields.description: A code that represents the type of name. Refer to HL7 Table 0200 - Name type for valid values. See Section 2.A.88.7, " Name Type Code (ID) ".

fields.name: Identifier Check Digit
fields.description: The check digit in this data type is not an add-on produced by the message processor. It is the check digit that is part of the identifying number used in the sending application. If the sending application does not include a self-generated check digit in the identifying number, this component should be valued null.

fields.name: Check Digit Scheme
fields.description: Contains the code identifying the check digit scheme employed.

fields.name: Identifier Type Code
fields.description: A code corresponding to the type of identifier. In some cases, this code may be used as a qualifier to the <assigning authority> component. Refer to HL7 Table 0203 - Identifier type for suggested values.

fields.name: Assigning Facility
fields.description: The place or location identifier where the identifier was first assigned to the person. This component is not an inherent part of the identifier but rather part of the history of the identifier: as part of this data type, its existence is a convenience for certain intercommunicating systems.

fields.name: Name Representation Code
fields.description: Different <name/address types> and representations of the same <name/address> should be described by repeating of this field, with different values of the <name/address type> and/or <name/address representation> component.

fields.name: Name Context
fields.description: This component is used to designate the context in which a name is used. The main use case is in Australian healthcare for indigenous patients who prefer to use different names when attending different healthcare institutions. Another use case occurs in the US where health practitioners can be licensed under slightly different names and the reporting of the correct name is vital for administrative purposes. Refer to User-defined Table 0448 - Name context for suggested values.

fields.name: Name Validity Range
fields.description: Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to XCN.19 Effective Date and XCN.20 Expiration Date instead. This component cannot be fully expressed and has been identified as v 2.4 erratum.This component contains the start and end date/times that define the period during which this name was valid. See section 2.A.20, " DR - date/time range " for description of subcomponents.

fields.name: Name Assembly Order
fields.description: A code that represents the preferred display order of the components of this person name. Refer to HL7 Table 0444 - Name Assembly Order for valid values.

fields.name: Effective Date
fields.description: The first date, if known, on which the address is valid and active.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.description: The last date, if known, on which the address is valid and active.

fields.name: Professional Suffix
fields.description: Used to specify an abbreviation, or a string of abbreviations denoting qualifications that support the persons profession, (e.g., licenses, certificates, degrees, affiliations with professional societies, etc.). The Professional Suffix normally follows the Family Name when the Person Name is used for display purposes. Please note that this component is an unformatted string and is used for display purposes only. Detailed information regarding the contents of Professional Suffix is obtained using appropriate segments in Chapter 15, Personnel Management.

fields.name: Assigning Jurisdiction
fields.description: The geo-political body that assigned the identifier in component 1.

fields.name: Assigning Agency Or Department
fields.description: The agency or department that assigned the identifier in component 1.

data_types.type_name: Extended Composite Name and Identification Number for Organizations
data_types.description: This data type is used in fields (e.g., PV2-23, NK1-13, PD1-3, OBR-44) to specify the name and ID number of an organization.

definition.name: Extended Composite Name and Identification Number for Organizations
definition.description: This data type is used in fields (e.g., PV2-23, NK1-13, PD1-3, OBR-44) to specify the name and ID number of an organization.

fields.name: Organization Name
fields.description: The name of the specified organization.

fields.name: Organization Name Type Code
fields.description: A code that represents the type of name i.e., legal name, display name. Refer to User-defined Table 0204 - Organizational Name Type for suggested values.

fields.name: Id Number
fields.description: This component has been retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. It is recommended to use component 10 Organization identifier that accommodates alphanumeric identifiers.

fields.name: Check Digit
fields.description: The check digit in this data type is not an add-on produced by the message processor. It is the check digit that is part of the identifying number used in the sending application. If the sending application does not include a self-generated check digit in the identifying number, this component should be valued null.

fields.name: Check Digit Scheme
fields.description: Contains the code identifying the check digit scheme employed.

fields.name: Assigning Authority
fields.description: The assigning authority is a unique identifier of the system (or organization or agency or department) that creates the data. Assigning authorities are unique across a given HL7 implementation. Refer to User-defined Table 0363 - Assigning Authority for suggested values.

fields.name: Identifier Type Code
fields.description: A code corresponding to the type of identifier. In some cases, this code may be used as a qualifier to the "Assigning authority" component. Refer to HL7 Table 0203 - Identifier type for suggested values.

fields.name: Assigning Facility
fields.description: The place or location identifier where the identifier was first assigned to the person. This component is not an inherent part of the identifier but rather part of the history of the identifier: as part of this data type, its existence is a convenience for certain intercommunicating systems.

fields.name: Name Representation Code
fields.description: Different <name/address types> and representations of the same <name/address> should be described by repeating of this field, with different values of the <name/address type> and/or <name/address representation> component.

fields.name: Organization Identifier
fields.description: This component contains the sequence of characters (the code) that uniquely identifies the item being referenced by XON.1 Organization Name. This component replaces XON.3 ID Number as of v 2.5.

fields.name: Family Name
fields.description: This component allows full specification of the surname of a person. Where appropriate, it differentiates the person's own surname from that of the person's partner or spouse, in cases where the person's name may contain elements from either name. It also permits messages to distinguish the surname prefix (such as "van" or "de") from the surname root. See section 2.A.30, "FN - family name".

fields.name: Given Name
fields.description: First name.

fields.name: Second And Further Given Names Or Initials Thereof
fields.description: Multiple middle names may be included by separating them with spaces.

fields.name: Suffix (e.g., Jr Or Iii)
fields.description: Used to specify a name suffix (e.g., Jr. or III).

fields.name: Prefix (e.g., Dr)
fields.description: Used to specify a name prefix (e.g., Dr.).

fields.name: Degree (e.g., Md)
fields.description: Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. See Professional Suffix component.

fields.name: Name Type Code
fields.description: A code that represents the type of name. Refer to HL7 Table 0200 - Name type for valid values.

fields.name: Name Representation Code
fields.description: Different <name/address types> and representations of the same <name/address> should be described by repeating of this field, with different values of the <name/address type> and/or <name/address representation> component.

fields.name: Name Context
fields.description: This component is used to designate the context in which a name is used. The main use case is in Australian healthcare for indigenous patients who prefer to use different names when attending different healthcare institutions. Another use case occurs in the US where health practitioners can be licensed under slightly different names and the reporting of the correct name is vital for administrative purposes. Refer to User-defined Table 0448 - Name context for suggested values.

fields.name: Name Validity Range
fields.description: This component cannot be fully expressed. Identified as v 2.4 erratum. Retained for backward compatibility only as of v 2.5. Refer to Effective Date and Expiration Date components.This component contains the start and end date/times, which define the period during which this name was valid. See section 2.A.20, "DR - date/time range" for description of subcomponents.

fields.name: Name Assembly Order
fields.description: A code that represents the preferred display order of the components of this person name. Refer to HL7 0444 - Name assembly order for valid values.

fields.name: Effective Date
fields.description: The first date, if known, on which the person name is valid and active.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.description: The last date, if known, on which the person name is valid and active.

fields.name: Professional Suffix
fields.description: Used to specify an abbreviation, or a string of abbreviations denoting qualifications that support the persons profession, (e.g., licenses, certificates, degrees, affiliations with professional societies, etc.). The Professional Suffix normally follows the Family Name when the Person Name is used for display purposes. Please note that this component is an unformatted string and is used for display purposes only. Detailed information regarding the contents of Professional Suffix is obtained using appropriate segments in Chapter 15, Personnel Management.

fields.name: Telephone Number
fields.description: This component has been retained for backward compatibility only as of version 2.3. Specifies the telephone number in a predetermined format that includes an optional extension, beeper number and comment.Format: [NNN] [(999)]999-9999 [X99999] [B99999] [C any text]

fields.name: Telecommunication Use Code
fields.description: A code that represents a specific use of a telecommunicationnumber. Refer to HL7 Table 0201 - Telecommunication use code for valid values.

fields.name: Telecommunication Equipment Type
fields.description: A code that represents the type of telecommunicationequipment. Refer to HL7 Table 0202 - Telecommunication equipment type for valid values.

fields.name: Extension
fields.description: Contains comments with respect to the telephone number.

fields.name: Any Text
fields.description: The characters established within a companys internal telephone system network used as a prefix to the Extension component for internal dialing. Note that the use of Extension Prefix requires that the Extension component be valued and that digits, as well as special characters (e.g., *, #) may be used.

fields.name: Extension Prefix
fields.description: The characters established within a companys internal telephone system used in place of the (external) telephone number to facilitate calling because its length is shorter than that of the telephone number. Note that digits, as well as special characters (e.g., *, #) may be used.

fields.name: Speed Dial Code
fields.description: An expression of the telephone number as an unparsible string.

fields.name: Unformatted Telephone Number
fields.description: An expression of the telephone number as an unparsible stringExample: |^^^^^^^^^^^1-800-Dentist|
